evaluations.query: Fill out a multi-step form and submit it using agent-browser
expected_behavior: Uses snapshot -i to discover interactive elements
References elements by @e# refs, not CSS selectors
Re-snapshots after each navigation or significant DOM change
Waits for networkidle after form submission

evaluations.query: Extract the main article text from a web page
expected_behavior: Uses targeted ref extraction (get text @e#) over full body
Removes noise elements (nav, header, footer, ads) before extraction
Falls back to eval with querySelector for custom extraction
Caches extracted content to avoid re-scraping

evaluations.query: Capture content from a login-protected documentation site
expected_behavior: Navigates to login page and discovers form with snapshot
Fills credentials from environment variables, never hardcoded
Saves authenticated state with state save for reuse
Uses headed mode for OAuth/SSO flows requiring user interaction

evaluations.query: Scrape multiple pages without overwhelming the server
expected_behavior: Adds delays between page navigations to avoid overwhelming the server
Implements exponential backoff on 429 responses
Limits concurrent connections and uses retry with backoff on failed pages
Monitors and adjusts rate based on server responses

evaluations.query: Crawl a website for content extraction
expected_behavior: Checks robots.txt before crawling to respect site crawl policies
Respects Terms of Service and site policies
Uses identifiable user-agent string for transparent bot identification
Limits crawl scope to necessary pages only

evaluations.query: Create a trace recording to debug a browser automation issue
expected_behavior: Loads auth state via vault before starting trace to avoid capturing credentials
Uses trace start/stop to capture only the relevant interaction
Reviews console and errors output before redirecting to files
Stores debug output in /tmp/ or ephemeral directories, not project dirs

evaluations.query: Test a responsive web app on mobile devices
expected_behavior: Uses --device flag with specific device name for emulation
Verifies viewport dimensions after device emulation is set
Tests both --color-scheme dark and light modes
Checks iOS Simulator is booted before using --ios-simulator flag

evaluations.query: Find and click a button by its visible text label
expected_behavior: Uses find command with visible text instead of fragile @ref numbers
Uses find --role to narrow search by ARIA role when text is ambiguous
Falls back to snapshot -i when find returns multiple matches
Uses highlight to visually verify the correct element before clicking

evaluations.query: The page I'm navigating to shows an infinite loading spinner and never reaches networkidle. My automation hangs forever waiting for the page to load.
expected_behavior: Claude uses a timeout parameter on the wait command instead of waiting for networkidle indefinitely
Claude falls back to wait --load domcontentloaded when networkidle never triggers on the page
Claude does not let the automation hang forever without a timeout or circuit breaker mechanism
Claude checks for the presence of specific content elements instead of relying solely on network idle state

evaluations.query: I clicked a button using @e5 but after the page navigated, trying to interact with @e12 throws 'element not found'. All my saved element references are broken.
expected_behavior: Claude re-runs snapshot after every navigation or significant DOM change to refresh element references
Claude does not reuse stale @e# references from a previous page state after navigation events
Claude explains that element references are invalidated by page navigation and dynamic DOM updates
Claude uses find with visible text or ARIA role to re-locate elements after DOM changes instead of cached refs

evaluations.query: I'm recording a browser trace to debug a login flow but the trace file captures my username and password as I type them into the form fields.
expected_behavior: Claude loads authentication state from a saved vault before starting the trace recording
Claude never records traces during the credential entry phase of authentication flows
Claude stores trace output in /tmp/ or ephemeral directories and not in the project repository
Claude warns that trace recordings capture all DOM interactions including sensitive form field input
